transitions.summary:
## Task Completion Report: wf_product_definition

### Summary
Completed product scope definition for Android Gesture Recognition App.

### Deliverables Created
- **Artifact**: docs/product/01_product_scope.md
- Status: Created and ready for review

### Acceptance Criteria Status
- ✅ Clarify target users and key scenarios
  - Defined 3 user segments: Tech-savvy Early Adopters, Accessibility Users, General Consumers
  - Documented 3 detailed user scenarios with user stories
  
- ✅ Define MVP features and out-of-scope items
  - MVP Features: 6 core features including camera preview, hand detection, gesture recognition, visual feedback, action mapping, settings
  - MVP Gesture Set: 5 gestures (Swipe Left/Right/Up/Down, Open Palm)
  - Out-of-Scope: 13 features deferred to future versions (custom gestures, multi-user, AR overlay, etc.)

### Key Decisions Made
1. Focused MVP on 5 core gestures (vs 10 in PRD) for faster time-to-market
2. Single-hand detection only for MVP (vs 2 hands in PRD)
3. Rear camera only, no front camera in MVP
4. Basic enable/disable settings only, full customization deferred
5. Haptic feedback and accessibility features moved to v1.1

### Next Phase
Ready for wf_interaction_design phase - interaction designer can proceed with gesture flow specifications.

tasks.lastSummary:

transitions.summary:
Interaction flow document completed.

**Artifacts produced:**
- docs/ux/interaction/01_interaction_flow.md

**Document covers:**
1. End-to-end interaction flow with state machine design (6 states: LAUNCH, HOME, CAMERA_PREVIEW, GESTURE_ACTIVE, SETTINGS, ONBOARDING)
2. Gesture detection lifecycle with timing parameters (30fps, 150ms hold threshold, 50px swipe min distance, 800ms feedback duration)
3. Detailed flow diagrams for swipe gestures and open palm gesture
4. Visual feedback rules with 6 feedback types, color codes, and text messages
5. State transition rules and valid transition matrix
6. Error handling flows (permission denied, no hand detected, poor lighting)
7. Back navigation behavior
8. Acceptance criteria for end-to-end flow and gesture feedback

**Dependencies satisfied:**
- wf_product_definition (completed by product_owner)
- All acceptance criteria covered: end-to-end interaction flow, gesture interaction feedback rules

transitions.summary:
## UI Design Task Completed

### Artifact Produced
- **docs/ux/ui/01_ui_spec.md** - Complete UI specification document

### Content Summary
1. **Screen Specifications**: 5 screens (Splash, Home, Camera Preview, Settings, Onboarding) with detailed layouts
2. **Component Specifications**: Buttons, Cards, Gesture Overlay, Bottom Navigation
3. **Visual Design System**: Color palette, Typography (Material 3), Spacing system, Corner radius
4. **Animation Specs**: Screen transitions, Gesture feedback animations, Micro-interactions
5. **Accessibility**: Touch targets (48dp min), Color contrast (WCAG AA), Screen reader support, Reduced motion
6. **Responsive Design**: 3 breakpoints (Compact <600dp, Medium 600-840dp, Expanded >840dp)
7. **Dark Theme**: Color mapping, System preference detection, Manual toggle
8. **Error States**: Camera permission denied, No hand detected, Poor lighting

### Acceptance Criteria Met
- ✅ Define key screens and UI component rules
- ✅ Define accessibility and visual consistency baseline

### Dependencies
- Built upon docs/ux/interaction/01_interaction_flow.md (interaction design)

transitions.summary:
## Engineering Decomposition Complete

### Completed Deliverables

**Artifact Created**: `docs/engineering/01_dev_breakdown.md`

### Work Packages Defined (14 Total)

#### Android Development (5 packages)
- WP-ANDROID-001: Project Setup
- WP-ANDROID-002: Camera Integration
- WP-ANDROID-003: UI Implementation
- WP-ANDROID-004: Settings & Persistence
- WP-ANDROID-005: Integration & Polish

#### ML Engineering (4 packages)
- WP-ML-001: ML Pipeline Setup
- WP-ML-002: Hand Detection
- WP-ML-003: Gesture Classification
- WP-ML-004: Model Optimization

#### QA (3 packages)
- WP-QA-001: Test Planning
- WP-QA-002: Test Automation
- WP-QA-003: Performance Testing

#### DevOps (2 packages)
- WP-DEV-001: CI/CD Pipeline
- WP-DEV-002: Build & Release

### Dependency Order
- Phase 1 (Weeks 1-2): Foundation - Project setup, ML infrastructure, test planning
- Phase 2 (Weeks 3-6): Core - Camera, hand detection, UI implementation
- Phase 3 (Weeks 7-8): ML & Integration - Gesture classification, settings
- Phase 4 (Weeks 9-12): Testing & Release - Optimization, performance, release

### Integration Checkpoints
- CP-1: Project Foundation (Week 2)
- CP-2: Camera & Hand Detection (Week 4)
- CP-3: Gesture Recognition (Week 6)
- CP-4: Feature Complete (Week 8)
- CP-5: Release Ready (Week 10)
- CP-6: Production Release (Week 12)

### Next Steps
- Task is ready for handoff to wf_engineering_execution phase
- Assign work packages to respective team leads (android_dev, ml_engineer, qa_engineer, devops_release)

   - Content: Complete Android gesture recognition app architecture including:
     - Module architecture (gesture-core, gesture-camera, gesture-ui)
     - Package structure
     - Key components (GestureEngine, HandTracker, GestureClassifier)
     - Technology stack (Jetpack, CameraX, MediaPipe, TensorFlow Lite)

transitions.summary:
## Task Completion Report: wf_quality_release

### Summary
Successfully created quality validation and release runbook artifacts for the Android Gesture Recognition Application.

### Artifacts Created
1. **docs/qa/01_test_plan.md** (10,560 bytes)
   - Test strategy with test pyramid approach
   - Test environment configuration (device matrix, emulators)
   - Feature test matrix covering all 10 gestures
   - Detailed test cases for camera, gesture recognition, settings, and performance
   - Non-functional requirements validation criteria
   - Sign-off criteria for release

2. **docs/release/01_release_runbook.md** (9,217 bytes)
   - Pre-release checklist (development, QA, build verification)
   - Release types (alpha, beta, production)
   - Step-by-step release process with commands
   - Distribution channels (Google Play, Firebase)
   - Comprehensive rollback strategy with triggers and procedures
   - Hotfix process for critical issues
   - Monitoring and alerts configuration

### Acceptance Criteria Met
- [x] Define test strategy and key acceptance tests
- [x] Define release steps and rollback strategy

### Verification
- Test plan includes all 10 gesture types with accuracy targets ≥75%
- Release runbook includes complete rollback procedure with code revert steps
- Both documents follow the workflow artifact format requirements
